page: Microsoft Azure E4-2ds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EDSv5 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 1 physical core and 2 dedicated vCPUs via hyperthreading, providing 2 threads per core. It features 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is bundled with a 154 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. It operates under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and does not include any GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category. However, multi-core performance is limited, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores ranking in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with both read and write large-block uncached tests showing poor performance. Cryptographic and compression tasks perform at weak to average levels, as evidenced by Passmark CPU encryption and single-threaded gzip compression benchmarks.

The primary tradeoff of the E4-2ds_v5 is its high memory capacity relative to its limited processing power. While the 32.0 GB RAM allocation provides excellent resource density for memory-bound tasks, the dual-vCPU configuration restricts parallel processing efficiency, as shown by poor database operations and Redis SET benchmarks. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this server is suitable for workloads requiring a large memory footprint without intensive computational demands. Appropriate workloads include small-scale databases, caching layers, memory-intensive text processing, and lightweight web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure E4-2ds_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 1 physical core and 2 dedicated vCPUs, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM and a 154 GB local disk. While single-core benchmarks for CPU and text processing fall in the average range, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are comparatively weak, ranking in the bottom 10%. This resource profile presents a clear tradeoff: high memory density alongside limited parallel processing power. Consequently, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-compute workloads. It is best suited for small-scale databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive text processing applications that do not require high multi-threaded performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4-2ds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, 2 dedicated vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a 154 GB local disk, designed for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4-2ds_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 2 vCPUs, 32.0 GB RAM, and 154 GB local storage, suited for low-compute, memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine offering high RAM-to-vCPU density and local storage for memory-bound, low-compute workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 1 physical core
High memory density with 32.0 GB RAM providing 16.0 GB per core
Bundled 154 GB local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average single-core CPU and text processing performance
Limited multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth
Suited for small databases, caching, and memory-bound applications
